_note: Refreshed by the refresh-industry-feed scheduled task. Space, EO, maritime, climate, environment headlines with industry-insider snark. Rotate sources; do not oversample one outlet.

items.headline: NordSpace nets Canadian defense funding for VLEO satellite development
items.snark: A $183,000 study contract is not a constellation foundation, it's a reimbursable, and the press release knows it.

items.headline: Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes
items.snark: The 90-minutes-to-minutes story keeps getting reheated because the real bottleneck is the human in a SCIF, and nobody has figured out how to compress that.

items.headline: OMB suggests NOAA scale back plans for geostationary satellites
items.snark: Killing the GeoXO instrument contracts after a decade of NOAA building the customer base is the most expensive way an agency can try to save money this year.

items.headline: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems add mid-class Resolute satellite bus
items.snark: The 2-to-4 kW class is the missing rung Boeing has needed for a decade; whether they can actually price it against Lockheed and Airbus is the part the press release skips.

items.headline: New Glenn grounded and AST SpaceMobile satellite lost after launch anomaly
items.snark: Insurance claims are the only commercial product New Glenn has shipped this quarter, and AST will spend the next twelve months pretending the unit economics still work.

items.headline: At high seas treaty summit, a dispute over fisheries managers' role in conservation
items.snark: RFMOs writing themselves into the BBNJ implementation rules is the move anyone who has watched WCPFC for two decades would have called from the airport.

items.headline: Talks to reduce funding for overfishing remain stalled at WTO meeting
items.snark: Three countries blocking a subsidies deal everyone else wants is the WTO running on schedule, not malfunctioning.

items.headline: How the US rebuilt a collapsed fishery
items.snark: Twenty-five years from disaster declaration to recovered stocks is the real timescale of US fisheries management, and the current NOAA budget posture suggests nobody wants to run that experiment again.

items.headline: Cropped 22 April 2026: Global food 'catastrophe', BECCS emissions, UK solar farm controversy
items.snark: BECCS booked as removals in the same week Hormuz volatility threatens 20 to 45 percent of global agrifood inputs is why the food and climate communities have stopped reading each other's papers.

items.headline: DeBriefed 10 April 2026: Worst energy crisis 'ever', India withdraws COP33 bid, drag artists and climate change
items.snark: India pulling its COP33 bid will turn out to be the most consequential climate-diplomacy story of the quarter, and the energy-crisis headlines will continue to bury it.

items.headline: Indigenous health can't be separated from environmental health, leaders tell UN
items.snark: Indigenous health and environmental health being inseparable is obvious right up until donors and health ministries are asked to fund the work that way.

items.headline: Climate adaptation funding is scarce. Private investors could help.
items.snark: The 'private capital will close the adaptation gap' pitch keeps getting recycled because the only honest alternative, public balance sheets that size, is politically dead.
